core_takeaway: Agent_1 anchored Stone but conceded the high-value Jewel path to Agent_5, so the final Plan Alpha consensus favored the last position.
why_or_counterexample: The first speaker gained the stable Stone allocation, but Agent_5's Jewel claim became the fast-consensus default and was worth far more after discounting.

phase_notes.public_discussion: Public discussion repeatedly framed Plan Alpha as the fast default: Jewel to Agent_5, Stone to Agent_1, Pencil to Agent_4, Quill to Agent_2, Apple to Agent_3. Agent_1's Stone anchor survived, but Agent_5's Jewel claim dominated the high-value outcome.
phase_notes.private_thinking: Private thinking is available. Agents repeatedly identified Jewel as the central lever, Plan Alpha as the default, Plan A/Jewel-to-Agent_3 as the slower fallback, and Plan Beta as a last resort.
phase_notes.formal_proposals: Formal proposals are available. Early rounds contained competing self-interested alternatives, but by round 4 all five formal proposals were the same Plan Alpha allocation.
phase_notes.private_voting: Private voting is available. Rounds 1-3 show payoff-driven rejections and failed alternatives; in round 4 all agents accepted the identical Plan Alpha allocation to close.
phase_notes.reflection: Reflection is available. Reflections converge on the same diagnosis: Jewel's destination blocked consensus, while Stone and Pencil were stable anchors used to make Plan Alpha pass.

mechanisms.theme: Jewel-to-Agent_5 became the accepted high-value default
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_1 wanted Jewel, but Agent_5's later public claim framed Jewel-to-5 as both personally important and efficient. Agent_1 then publicly endorsed the Plan Alpha structure that routed Jewel to Agent_5.

evidence.quote: top targets are Jewel and Quill
Jewel goes to the highest-valuing recipient (me)

mechanisms.theme: First position did anchor Stone for Agent_1, but only as a mid-value consolation
mechanisms.explanation: The main first-position benefit was that Stone stayed assigned to Agent_1 across the accepted plans. That helped Agent_1 avoid being shut out, but Stone was much less valuable than Agent_5's Jewel.

mechanisms.theme: Speed and stable-anchor pressure defeated Agent_1's better alternatives
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_1 privately preferred higher-payoff Apple-plus-Stone alternatives in early voting, but those alternatives could not survive group resistance. By the final round, closure pressure made Agent_1 accept discounted Stone-only utility.

evidence.quote: Jewel remains the main sticking point
Accept to lock in a fast close

mechanisms.theme: Round-4 convergence locked in Agent_5's superior discounted utility
mechanisms.explanation: By round 4, every formal proposal used the same allocation and all votes accepted it. Since the shared allocation gave Agent_1 Stone and Agent_5 Jewel, the identical consensus process produced a much higher final utility for Agent_5.

mechanisms.theme: Private and reflective reasoning reinforced Plan Alpha rather than Agent_1's top preference
mechanisms.explanation: The private-thought and reflection sections did not reveal a hidden first-mover advantage. They repeatedly diagnosed Jewel as the blocker and recommended Plan Alpha as the default route, which preserved Agent_1's Stone but routed the decisive prize to Agent_5.

evidence.quote: Use Plan Alpha as the default fast end
Jewel remains the core sticking point
Adopt Plan Alpha as the default quick end
